_source: Central Bank of Ireland Mortgage Measures — effective 1 January 2023

firstTimeBuyer.label: First-time buyer — max loan is 4x gross annual income

secondSubsequentBuyer.label: Second or subsequent buyer — max loan is 3.5x gross annual income

qualifyingQuestions.question: Are you a first-time buyer, or have you owned a property before?
qualifyingQuestions.purpose: Determines which LTV and LTI limits apply

qualifyingQuestions.question: What price property are you looking at?
qualifyingQuestions.purpose: Used to calculate required loan and LTV

qualifyingQuestions.question: How much deposit do you have saved?
What is your gross annual income? If applying jointly, what is the combined income?
qualifyingQuestions.purpose: Used to calculate maximum borrowing under LTI rules

qualifyingQuestions.question: Are you PAYE employed, self-employed, or a contractor?
qualifyingQuestions.purpose: Self-employed typically requires 2+ years of accounts. PAYE is strongest.

qualifyingQuestions.question: Do you have any existing loans, car finance, or credit card debt?
qualifyingQuestions.purpose: Affects affordability assessment — reduces effective borrowing capacity

qualifyingQuestions.question: Have you ever missed a loan or mortgage repayment?
qualifyingQuestions.purpose: Any missed payments within the last 5 years is a significant red flag for lenders

hot: Deposit meets or exceeds the minimum required for their buyer type
Required loan does not exceed the LTI limit for their buyer type
No missed payments or credit issues
warm: Meets LTV and LTI rules but is self-employed (needs 2+ years accounts)
Marginally short on deposit (within 5% of requirement)
Has minor existing debts but income still supports the loan
No credit issues but contractor employment status
cold: Deposit is significantly below the minimum required
Required loan exceeds the LTI limit
Missed payments in the last 5 years
Income does not support the required mortgage

additionalNotes: Lenders may grant LTI exemptions — up to 20% of new FTB lending can exceed 4x (some lenders allow up to 4.5x)
Lenders may grant LTV exemptions in limited cases
Minimum income thresholds vary by lender — typically €30,000+ for single applicants
Maximum loan term is generally 35 years (some lenders 30)
Age at end of mortgage term typically cannot exceed 70
